source_plan: Chat-approved 9-task prepilot plan: agents, GERENTE, Discord bridge, private profile separation, provider/model lock, public repo alignment, permissions, reference repo execution and prepilot battery.
coverage_rule: A task is counted as ready only when it has at least one implementation artifact stronger than documentation: schema, template, script, test, worker registry, worker profile, binding, permission matrix, runtime adapter or validation artifact.

tasks.plain_goal: Classify vFinal agents as required core, optional pack, private, backlog or remove.
tasks.implementation_summary: The public roster, registry, profiles and capability packs separate core workers from optional packs and document ownership for each canonical stage.

evidence_refs.covers: human-readable worker classification and vFinal ownership
machine-readable worker registry
worker purpose, authority, contracts and evidence requirements
optional modular packs and promotion rules
profile integrity checks
capability-pack contract checks

tasks.remaining_limit: The public repo intentionally does not include private runtime profile directories or private product profiles.

tasks.plain_goal: Create the correct GERENTE for Discord without turning it into a worker executor.
tasks.implementation_summary: The GERENTE is represented as a dedicated gateway profile assignment, while execution remains with Hermes workers and the Kanban adapter.

evidence_refs.covers: dedicated overkill-factory-gerente gateway assignment
dedicated gateway profile is present and private product profile is not the factory gateway
real runtime Discord gateway configuration and restart receipt
fresh public-safe read-only runtime status for GERENTE and Discord gateway
Portuguese operator setup and GERENTE usage model

tasks.remaining_limit: Raw Discord bot ids, token refs, server ids and runtime paths are private evidence and stay outside the public repo.

tasks.plain_goal: Make Discord Control Tower Bridge an official part of the factory.
tasks.implementation_summary: The bridge is a named worker with registry, profile binding, bridge-health schema and production readiness proof; it maps events but does not become source of truth.

evidence_refs.covers: official bridge worker entry
Hermes binding, receipt field and queue policy
contractual bridge health format
Control Tower mapping, registration and bridge health proof rules
public-safe production readiness receipt with private evidence redacted

tasks.remaining_limit: Discord remains cockpit only; Hermes/Kanban remains the durable source of truth.

tasks.plain_goal: Keep the factory Discord gateway out of the private project profile.
tasks.implementation_summary: The live audit records that the private product profile is not used as the factory gateway and that the factory gateway has its own dedicated profile.

evidence_refs.covers: private product profile is outside factory gateway scope
factory gateway profile assignment is separate from workers and private product profiles
real runtime update receipt for Discord Control Tower activation
private product profile is not the active factory gateway

tasks.remaining_limit: The private project profile name and raw runtime config are intentionally not copied into public artifacts.

tasks.plain_goal: Lock the canonical provider/model and prove auth per profile.
tasks.implementation_summary: The provider/model audit and profile smoke prove the public worker set and dedicated gateway profile against the canonical model with private details redacted.

evidence_refs.covers: provider/model audit contract
canonical provider/model matrix and exceptions
live profile smoke matrix
current read-only runtime status confirms profile list and gateway state
worker/profile validation rules

tasks.remaining_limit: Live auth proof can drift after credential or model changes; rerun the audit before a real pilot.

tasks.plain_goal: Align the public repo, real Hermes behavior and canonical vFinal documentation.
tasks.implementation_summary: The public docs, canonical stage coverage record and open-source tests connect what the canonical document promises to repo artifacts and setup instructions.

evidence_refs.covers: canonical stage promise-to-implementation map
canonical stages cannot be covered by documentation alone
open-source documentation expectations
public setup path for another Hermes installation
public project entrypoint
prepilot backlog and future work kept outside the canonical document

tasks.remaining_limit: The external canonical narrative document remains outside the public repo; this receipt verifies repo implementation coverage.

tasks.plain_goal: Separate permissions for GERENTE, bridge, builders, reviewers, security and release.
tasks.implementation_summary: A dedicated permission-class matrix separates gateway, bridge, executor, reviewer, security, release and human-gate authority.

evidence_refs.covers: permission class contract
worker-to-permission-class mapping
permission matrix regression checks
plain-language permission model

tasks.remaining_limit: Runtime enforcement still depends on Hermes profile installation and adapter gates in the target operator environment.

tasks.plain_goal: Study reference agents/skills, compare against current agents, then execute improvements and add new modular coverage where useful.
tasks.implementation_summary: The study produced executed changes: work-unit, reviewer-selection, QA verification and agent-eval-result contracts, plus stronger agent profile requirements.

evidence_refs.covers: reference repo study and execution log
study conclusions became contracts and profile requirements
work-unit contract introduced from the study
reviewer selection contract introduced from the study
QA mode coverage contract introduced from the study
agent eval result contract introduced from the study
profile changes for decomposition, reviewer, QA and skill evaluation workers

tasks.remaining_limit: New specialist packs should continue to be added through eval, permission and binding gates rather than copied wholesale from any reference repository.

tasks.plain_goal: Run the complete prepilot battery before sending a real pilot paper.
tasks.implementation_summary: The validation battery covers routing, blockers, done-transition reconciliation, public JSON validation, worker profile checks and production readiness receipts.

evidence_refs.covers: multi-scenario factory validation battery
battery result record
aggregate production readiness receipt builder
public-safe aggregate readiness record
schema validation for public JSON artifacts
secret-safety scan
public-safety scan

tasks.remaining_limit: The pilot still needs the operator to submit a real paper/input and approve any human gates that appear.

limits: This is a public-safe readiness receipt; it intentionally omits raw private Discord ids, runtime ids, private paths, tokens and logs.
PASS means the agreed prepilot setup has contract and evidence coverage; it does not mean every future product can skip its own planning, access, security, review or release gates.
Hermes remains the source of truth. Discord is an operator cockpit and approval surface, not the durable state store.
